lesson.menu_title: How Much, How Many — Talking About Quantities in HR Reports and Workforce Data
lesson.menu_desc: Talking about quantities with quantifiers and countable/uncountable nouns -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- How Much, How Many -- Quantifiers and Countable/Uncountable
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 8 -- How Much, How Many

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Here is a quick summary of our workforce data before the report. Right now we have several open vacancies, about eight in total, and we received many applicants last month, so that is good news. However, we do not have enough staff in the night shift, so the team is doing too much overtime. There is also a lot of absence this quarter, more than usual, and I think there is a connection. On the positive side, we collected a lot of feedback from the exit interviews, and the data is very clear. We have plenty of resources for training, but we need a little more time to organize it. In one line: many applicants, not enough staff, and too much overtime. Let's fix the shortage first. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana from London, with a quick note on staffing before the board meeting. We have a shortage of engineers across three regions. There are not many senior applicants in the market, and we do not have much time before the deadline. My team has collected a lot of data, and the picture is clear: we have too many open vacancies and too few recruiters. We also have very little budget left for agencies this quarter. My recommendation is simple. We need a few quick wins: first, reduce overtime, because there is too much of it; second, give the recruiters more resources; and third, ask for a little extra headcount. If we do this, we are going to close most of the gap. I am sending you the numbers now. Thank you.
